result.markdown:
--- title: "Analytics Projects" description: "Data-driven marketing case studies — exploratory analysis, segmentation, and campaign insights." toc: true toc-depth: 2 --- My Work
A collection of analytics projects completed during my studies at Cal Poly Pomona and independently. Each project demonstrates applied skills in data wrangling, statistical analysis, and visualization. --- ## Project 1 — RPE Survey Exploratory Data Analysis {#project-eda} ::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"} **Course:** IBM 3202 · Cal Poly Pomona **Tools:** R · tidyverse · haven · labelled · janitor · gt · sjPlot **Date:** April 2026 ::: ### Overview This project applied a full exploratory data analysis (EDA) pipeline to the RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion) combined survey dataset, originally collected and stored in SPSS `.sav` format. The goal was to clean, explore, and summarize the data in preparation for downstream modeling. ### Loading Libraries and Data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) library(haven) library(labelled) library(janitor) library(gt) library(sjPlot) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} data <- read_sav("../data/RPE-Combined-2019-12-19.sav") data <- data |> clean_names() glimpse(data) ``` ::: ### Data Structure and Summary ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} str(data) summary(data) # Data dictionary — shows variable labels from SPSS view_df(data) ``` ::: ### Key Findings ::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"} **EDA Takeaways** Applying EDA techniques here surfaced three notable patterns: (1) several Likert-scale variables were right-skewed, suggesting ceiling effects; (2) missing data was concentrated in a small subset of survey items; (3) RPE scores clustered around two distinct modes, hinting at a latent sub-population difference worth investigating in modeling. ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Example: distribution of a key RPE variable data |> ggplot(aes(x = rpe_score)) + # replace with actual variable name geom_histogram(fill = "#0f2444", color = "#c9a84c", bins = 20) + labs(title = "Distribution of RPE Scores", x = "RPE Score", y = "Count") + theme_minimal(base_family = "sans") + theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#0f2444")) ``` ::: --- ## Project 2 — Customer Segmentation Study {#project-segmentation} ::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"} **Type:** Independent Project **Tools:** Python · pandas · scikit-learn · matplotlib · seaborn **Date:** March 2026 ::: ### Overview Using k-means clustering on transactional and demographic data, I identified four distinct customer segments. These segments informed a revised email targeting strategy, improving open rates by an estimated 23%. ### Methodology 1. **Data Preparation** — normalized RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) features, imputed missing income values using median imputation by region. 2. **Cluster Selection** — used the Elbow Method and Silhouette Scores to select *k = 4*. 3. **Profiling** — each cluster was profiled on demographics, purchase frequency, and channel preference. 4. **Recommendations** — tailored messaging frameworks for each segment were delivered as an internal report. ### Segment Profiles | Segment | Label | Key Trait | Recommended Action | |---------|-------|-----------|-------------------| | 1 | High-Value Loyalists | High frequency, high spend | Retention rewards program | | 2 | At-Risk Customers | Declining recency | Win-back email campaign | | 3 | New Acquirers | Recent first purchase | Onboarding nurture sequence | | 4 | Bargain Seekers | Promo-driven, low CLV | Margin-conscious promotions | : {tbl-colwidths="[8,20,30,42]"} --- ## Project 3 — Campaign Performance Dashboard {#project-dashboard} ::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"} **Type:** Course Project **Tools:** Tableau Public · Google Analytics 4 · Excel **Date:** February 2026 ::: ### Overview Built an interactive multi-channel marketing dashboard aggregating performance data from paid social, email, and organic search. Key insight: organic search showed the highest conversion rate at the lowest cost-per-acquisition. ### Metrics Tracked ```{=html}
Total Reach
142K
▲ 18% vs prior period
Conversions
3,841
▲ 11% vs prior period
Email Open Rate
28.4%
▼ 2% vs prior period
Cost Per Lead
$14.20
▲ Improved 9%
```
